2606.22305.title: Learning at the Right Pace: Adaptive Data Scheduling Improves LLM Reinforcement Learning
2606.22305.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve remarkable reasoning capabilities through reinforcement learning (RL) post-training. However, existing RL post-training commonly relies on uniform data sampling, which ignores the semantic structure of the training data and the changing capability of the training policy. To address these limitations, we propose Adaptive Data Scheduling (ADS), a dual-level data scheduling framework for pacing RL post-training that replaces uniform sampling with an adaptive distribution over semantic clusters and policy-boundary sample selection. At the cluster level, ADS organizes samples according to semantic patterns and maintains an adaptive inter-cluster distribution to solidify current training progress. At the sample level, ADS performs intra-cluster scheduling to continuously sample policy-boundary samples, which provides informative relative advantages. Experimental results across three LLMs and seven reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that ADS improves average accuracy by 5.2% over Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). Notably, ADS consistently improves RL methods with different objective designs, highlighting its potential as a general data scheduling strategy for LLM RL post-training. The source code is available at: https://github.com/Richard-zrx/ADS.
2606.22305.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）通過強化學習（RL）後訓練實現了卓越的推理能力。<lb/>然而，現有的RL後訓練通常依賴於均勻數據抽樣，這忽略了訓練數據的語義結構和訓練策略的變化能力。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，我們提出了自適應數據調度（ADS），這是一種雙層數據調度框架，用於調整RL後訓練的速度，將均勻抽樣替換為基於語義集群的自適應分佈和策略邊界樣本選擇。<lb/>在集群層面，ADS根據語義模式組織樣本，並維持自適應的集群間分佈，以鞏固當前的訓練進展。<lb/>在樣本層面，ADS執行集群內調度，以持續抽樣策略邊界樣本，這提供了有意義的相對優勢。<lb/>在三個LLM和七個推理基準上的實驗結果表明，ADS相較於群體相對策略優化（GRPO）提高了平均準確率5.2%。<lb/>值得注意的是，ADS一致性地改善了具有不同目標設計的RL方法，突顯了其作為LLM RL後訓練通用數據調度策略的潛力。<lb/>源代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/Richard-zrx/ADS。
2606.22305.author: Zicheng Xu et.al.
2606.22305.authors: Zicheng Xu, Ruixuan Zhang, Yu-Neng Chuang, Xiuyi Lou, Hoang Anh Duy Le, Oren Gal, Alexander S. Szalay, Zhaozhuo Xu, Guanchu Wang, Vladimir Braverman
